items.title: Forest Green Rovers scrap women’s team to ‘concentrate resources’ on men’s side
items.summary: Men’s team relegated to National League in 2024 Club hope women’s football will return in future Forest Green Rovers have disbanded their women’s team for the 2026-27 season so that they can “concentrate their resources” on trying to return their men’s team to the English Football League. items.title: Koeman resigns, while Netherlands report racist abuse
items.summary: Ronald Koeman resigns as Netherlands manager after their World Cup exit, which led to "appalling" racist abuse aimed at players who missed penalties.

items.title: Ronald Koeman resigns as Netherlands head coach after World Cup loss to Morocco
items.summary: ‘We fell short. items.title: Garcia fails to qualify for Open; Nicholas leads Burnham & Berrow
items.summary: Sergio Garcia says he fell ill during qualifying for The Open, hindering his chance of qualifying for the tournament next month. James Nicholas qualifies by finishing top of the leaderboard in Burnham & Berrow.

items.title: Battling Serena suffers Wimbledon defeat on much-anticipated return
items.summary: Serena Williams suffered defeat to Maya Joint in her much-anticipated return to Wimbledon following a four-year absence after an epic battle on Centre Court.

items.summary: Paris St-Germain set their price for Bradley Barcola, Chelsea want Bournemouth striker Eli Junior Kroupi, Ayyoub Bouaddi is attracting interest, plus more.

items.title: Villa among four Premier League clubs fined by Uefa over squad-cost rules
items.summary: Aston Villa have been fined 22.5m euros (£19.4m) by Uefa for a "significant breach" of its squad-cost rule for 2025.

items.title: Rosenior nears management return at Paris FC
items.summary: Former Chelsea manager Liam Rosenior is set to return to football at French club Paris FC on a two-year contract, with the option of a further season.

items.title: Premier League winner, 40, takes two wickets and smashes six into a RIVER on cricket debut
items.summary: CHRISTIAN FUCHS had a cricket debut for the ages - just hours after leaving Newport County. The Austrian resigned from his role as manager of the League Two side after a mere SEVEN MONTHS at the Rodney Parade helm. items.title: Rangers sign Pandur from Hull as Butland nears Tigers move
items.summary: Rangers have signed goalkeeper Ivor Pandur from Hull City in a £6m deal.

items.title: ITV land rights to major sports tournament ahead of BBC and will show every England match until at least 2029
items.summary: ITV have landed the broadcast rights for the 2027 Rugby World Cup. And their deal with World Rugby is brilliant news for England fans. items.title: Ronald Koeman QUITS as Holland manager less than 24 hours after World Cup penalty shootout exit to Morocco
items.summary: RONALD KOEMAN has QUIT as Netherlands manager just hours after their World Cup exit. The Dutch were beaten on penalties by Morocco in the round of 32 on Monday. items.title: England fans to enjoy £1.50 pints in DR Congo game host city as stunned supporters say ‘It’s even cheaper than Benidorm’
items.summary: THREE Lions fans are set to smash the World Cup’s beer record as bars across Atlanta are selling pints for just £1.50 - slashed to 75p if it rains. A string of boozers in the Georgian city offer the cheapest alcohol prices in the US - and far cheaper than a pint of lager in...
